nodes.title: Short title, extent and commencement
nodes.summary: This section provides the short title, extent, and commencement details of the Act.

nodes.title: Definitions
nodes.summary: This section defines various terms used in the Act.

nodes.title: Construction of references
nodes.summary: This section explains how references to Magistrates should be construed.

nodes.title: Trial of offences under Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 and other laws
nodes.summary: This section outlines the trial process for offences under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 and other laws.

nodes.title: Saving
nodes.summary: This section states that the Act does not affect any special or local law unless specifically provided.

nodes.title: Classes of Criminal Courts
nodes.summary: This section lists the classes of criminal courts in every state.

nodes.title: Territorial divisions
nodes.summary: This section describes the territorial divisions for sessions and districts.

nodes.title: Court of Session
nodes.summary: This section details the establishment and jurisdiction of the Court of Session.

nodes.title: Courts of Judicial Magistrates
nodes.summary: This section provides for the establishment of Courts of Judicial Magistrates.

nodes.title: Chief Judicial Magistrate and Additional Chief Judicial Magistrate, etc.
nodes.summary: This section outlines the appointment and powers of Chief Judicial Magistrates.

nodes.title: Special Judicial Magistrates
nodes.summary: This section allows for the appointment of Special Judicial Magistrates.

nodes.title: Local Jurisdiction of Judicial Magistrates
nodes.summary: This section defines the local jurisdiction of Judicial Magistrates.

nodes.title: Subordination of Judicial Magistrates
nodes.summary: This section describes the subordination of Judicial Magistrates to higher authorities.

nodes.title: Executive Magistrates
nodes.summary: This section provides for the appointment and powers of Executive Magistrates.

nodes.title: Special Executive Magistrates
nodes.summary: This section allows for the appointment of Special Executive Magistrates.

nodes.title: Local Jurisdiction of Executive Magistrates
nodes.summary: This section defines the local jurisdiction of Executive Magistrates.

nodes.title: Subordination of Executive Magistrates
nodes.summary: This section describes the subordination of Executive Magistrates to higher authorities.

nodes.title: Public Prosecutors
nodes.summary: This section outlines the appointment and duties of Public Prosecutors.

nodes.title: Assistant Public Prosecutors
nodes.summary: This section provides for the appointment of Assistant Public Prosecutors.

nodes.title: Directorate of Prosecution
nodes.summary: This section establishes the Directorate of Prosecution and its functions.

nodes.title: Courts by which offences are triable
nodes.summary: This section specifies which courts can try offences under the Act.

nodes.title: Sentences which High Courts and Sessions Judges may pass
nodes.summary: This section outlines the sentencing powers of High Courts and Sessions Judges.

nodes.title: Sentences which Magistrates may pass
nodes.summary: This section details the sentencing powers of Magistrates.

nodes.title: Sentence of imprisonment in default of fine
nodes.summary: This section provides for imprisonment in default of payment of fines.

nodes.title: Sentence in cases of conviction of several offences at one trial
nodes.summary: This section discusses sentencing when multiple offences are tried at one trial.

nodes.title: Mode of conferring powers
nodes.summary: This section explains how powers are conferred under the Act.

nodes.title: Powers of officers appointed
nodes.summary: This section outlines the powers of officers appointed under the Act.

nodes.title: Withdrawal of powers
nodes.summary: This section provides for the withdrawal of powers conferred under the Act.

nodes.title: Powers of Judges and Magistrates exercisable by their successors-in-office
nodes.summary: This section allows successors-in-office to exercise the powers of their predecessors.

nodes.title: Powers of superior officers of police
nodes.summary: This section outlines the powers of superior police officers.

nodes.title: Public when to assist Magistrates and police
nodes.summary: This section mandates public assistance to Magistrates and police when required.

nodes.title: Aid to person, other than police officer, executing warrant
nodes.summary: This section allows for aid to persons executing warrants.

nodes.title: Public to give information of certain offences
nodes.summary: This section requires the public to report certain offences to authorities.

nodes.title: Duty of officers employed in connection with affairs of a village to make certain report
nodes.summary: This section mandates village officers to report certain information to authorities.

nodes.title: When police may arrest without warrant
nodes.summary: This section outlines the circumstances under which police can arrest without a warrant.

nodes.title: Procedure of arrest and duties of officer making arrest
nodes.summary: This section details the procedure and duties of officers during an arrest.

nodes.title: Designated police officer
nodes.summary: This section mandates the designation of police officers responsible for maintaining arrest records.

nodes.title: Right of arrested person to meet an advocate of his choice during interrogation
nodes.summary: This section grants arrested persons the right to meet an advocate during interrogation.

nodes.title: Arrest on refusal to give name and residence
nodes.summary: This section allows arrest if a person refuses to give their name and residence.

nodes.title: Arrest by private person and procedure on such arrest
nodes.summary: This section allows private persons to arrest offenders and outlines the procedure.

nodes.title: Arrest by Magistrate
nodes.summary: This section allows Magistrates to arrest offenders within their jurisdiction.

nodes.title: Protection of members of Armed Forces from arrest
nodes.summary: This section provides protection to Armed Forces members from arrest without government consent.

nodes.title: CHAPTER VI PROCESSES TO COMPEL APPEARANCE
nodes.summary: This chapter outlines the procedures for compelling the appearance of individuals in court, including the issuance of summons and warrants.

nodes.title: Form of summons
nodes.summary: Describes the format and requirements for issuing a summons by a court.

nodes.title: Summons how served
nodes.summary: Details the process for serving a summons, including personal delivery and electronic communication.

nodes.title: Service of summons on corporate bodies, firms, and societies
nodes.summary: Explains how summons are served to companies, corporations, and associations.

nodes.title: Service when persons summoned cannot be found
nodes.summary: Provides for alternative methods of serving summons when the person cannot be located.

nodes.title: Procedure when service cannot be effected as before provided
nodes.summary: Outlines the steps to take when a summons cannot be served through usual methods.

nodes.title: Service on Government servant
nodes.summary: Describes the procedure for serving summons to government employees.

nodes.title: Service of summons outside local limits
nodes.summary: Explains how to serve summons outside the local jurisdiction of the issuing court.

nodes.title: Proof of service in such cases and when serving officer not present
nodes.summary: Details how to prove service of summons when the serving officer is not present.

nodes.title: Service of summons on witness
nodes.summary: Allows for the service of summons to witnesses via electronic communication or registered post.

nodes.title: Form of warrant of arrest and duration
nodes.summary: Specifies the format and validity period of an arrest warrant.

nodes.title: Power to direct security to be taken
nodes.summary: Allows courts to endorse warrants with instructions for taking security for the appearance of the accused.

nodes.title: Warrants to whom directed
nodes.summary: Describes who can be directed to execute a warrant of arrest.

nodes.title: Warrant may be directed to any person
nodes.summary: Allows warrants to be directed to any person within the local jurisdiction for arrest.

nodes.title: Warrant directed to police officer
nodes.summary: Permits any police officer to execute a warrant directed to another officer.

nodes.title: Notification of substance of warrant
nodes.summary: Requires the executing officer to inform the person to be arrested of the warrant's substance.

nodes.title: Person arrested to be brought before Court without delay
nodes.summary: Mandates that arrested individuals be brought before a court promptly.

nodes.title: Where warrant may be executed
nodes.summary: Allows for the execution of warrants anywhere in India.

nodes.title: Warrant forwarded for execution outside jurisdiction
nodes.summary: Describes the process for forwarding warrants for execution outside the issuing court's jurisdiction.

nodes.title: Warrant directed to police officer for execution outside jurisdiction
nodes.summary: Details the procedure for executing warrants outside the local jurisdiction by police officers.

nodes.title: Procedure on arrest of person against whom warrant issued
nodes.summary: Outlines the steps to follow when a person is arrested outside the district where the warrant was issued.

nodes.title: Procedure by Magistrate before whom such person arrested is brought
nodes.summary: Describes the magistrate's role when a person arrested under a warrant is brought before them.

nodes.title: Proclamation for person absconding
nodes.summary: Allows courts to issue a proclamation for persons who have absconded to avoid arrest.

nodes.title: Attachment of property of person absconding
nodes.summary: Permits the attachment of property belonging to a person who has absconded.

nodes.title: Identification and attachment of property of proclaimed person
nodes.summary: Allows for the identification and attachment of property belonging to a proclaimed person.

nodes.title: Claims and objections to attachment
nodes.summary: Provides a process for third parties to claim or object to the attachment of property.

nodes.title: Release, sale and restoration of attached property
nodes.summary: Outlines the conditions under which attached property can be released, sold, or restored.

nodes.title: Appeal from order rejecting application for restoration of attached property
nodes.summary: Allows for appeals against orders rejecting applications for the restoration of attached property.

nodes.title: Issue of warrant in lieu of, or in addition to, summons
nodes.summary: Permits the issuance of a warrant instead of or in addition to a summons under certain conditions.

nodes.title: Power to take bond or bail bond for appearance
nodes.summary: Allows courts to take a bond or bail bond for a person's appearance in court.

nodes.title: Arrest on breach of bond or bail bond for appearance
nodes.summary: Provides for the arrest of individuals who breach their bond or bail bond conditions.

nodes.title: Provisions of this Chapter generally applicable to summons and warrants of arrest
nodes.summary: States that the provisions of this chapter apply to all summons and warrants of arrest.

nodes.title: CHAPTER VII PROCESSES TO COMPEL THE PRODUCTION OF THINGS
nodes.summary: This chapter covers the procedures for compelling the production of documents and other items in legal proceedings.

nodes.title: Summons to produce document or other thing
nodes.summary: Allows courts or police officers to issue summons for the production of documents or items necessary for investigations.

nodes.title: Procedure as to letters
nodes.summary: Describes the procedure for handling letters or parcels in the custody of postal authorities for legal purposes.

nodes.title: When search-warrant may be issued
nodes.summary: Outlines the conditions under which a search warrant may be issued by a court.

nodes.title: Search of place suspected to contain stolen property, forged documents, etc.
nodes.summary: Allows for the search of places suspected to contain stolen property or forged documents.

nodes.title: Power to declare certain publications forfeited and to issue search-warrants for same
nodes.summary: Empowers the state to declare certain publications forfeited and issue search warrants for them.

nodes.title: Application to High Court to set aside declaration of forfeiture
nodes.summary: Allows individuals to apply to the High Court to set aside a declaration of forfeiture of publications.

nodes.title: Search for persons wrongfully confined
nodes.summary: Permits the issuance of search warrants for persons believed to be wrongfully confined.

nodes.title: Power to compel restoration of abducted females
nodes.summary: Allows magistrates to order the restoration of abducted females to their lawful guardians.

nodes.title: Direction, etc., of search-warrants
nodes.summary: Applies certain procedural rules to the issuance and execution of search warrants.

nodes.title: Persons in charge of closed place to allow search
nodes.summary: Requires individuals in charge of premises to allow searches when presented with a warrant.

nodes.title: Disposal of things found in search beyond jurisdiction
nodes.summary: Describes the procedure for handling items found during searches conducted outside the issuing court's jurisdiction.

nodes.title: Recording of search and seizure through audio-video electronic means
nodes.summary: Mandates the recording of searches and seizures using audio-video electronic means.

nodes.title: Power of police officer to seize certain property
nodes.summary: Allows police officers to seize property suspected to be involved in criminal activity.

nodes.title: Attachment, forfeiture or restoration of property
nodes.summary: Provides for the attachment, forfeiture, or restoration of property related to criminal activity.

nodes.title: RECIPROCAL ARRANGEMENTS FOR ASSISTANCE IN CERTAIN MATTERS AND PROCEDURE FOR ATTACHMENT AND FORFEITURE OF PROPERTY
nodes.summary: This chapter outlines the procedures for reciprocal legal assistance between India and other countries, including the attachment and forfeiture of property related to criminal activities.

nodes.title: Definitions
nodes.summary: Defines key terms such as 'contracting State', 'proceeds of crime', and 'property' for the purposes of this chapter.

nodes.title: Letter of request to competent authority for investigation in a country or place outside India
nodes.summary: Allows Indian courts to request assistance from foreign authorities in gathering evidence for investigations.

nodes.title: Letter of request from a country or place outside India to a Court or an authority for investigation in India
nodes.summary: Describes the process for handling requests from foreign countries for assistance in investigations within India.

nodes.title: Assistance in securing transfer of persons
nodes.summary: Details the procedure for transferring individuals between India and contracting States for legal proceedings.

nodes.title: Assistance in relation to orders of attachment or forfeiture of property
nodes.summary: Outlines the process for attaching or forfeiting property in India or contracting States as part of legal assistance.

nodes.title: Identifying unlawfully acquired property
nodes.summary: Describes the steps for tracing and identifying property acquired through unlawful means.

nodes.title: Seizure or attachment of property
nodes.summary: Allows for the seizure or attachment of property suspected to be involved in criminal activities.

nodes.title: Management of properties seized or forfeited under this Chapter
nodes.summary: Provides for the appointment of an Administrator to manage seized or forfeited properties.

nodes.title: Notice of forfeiture of property
nodes.summary: Details the procedure for notifying individuals about the potential forfeiture of their property.

nodes.title: Forfeiture of property in certain cases
nodes.summary: Outlines the conditions under which property can be forfeited to the government.

nodes.title: Fine in lieu of forfeiture
nodes.summary: Allows for the imposition of a fine instead of forfeiture of property in certain cases.

nodes.title: Certain transfers to be null and void
nodes.summary: Declares certain property transfers null and void if they occur after a notice of forfeiture.

nodes.title: Procedure in respect of letter of request
nodes.summary: Specifies the procedure for handling letters of request between India and contracting States.

nodes.title: Application of this Chapter
nodes.summary: Allows the Central Government to specify conditions for the application of this chapter with contracting States.

nodes.title: SECURITY FOR KEEPING THE PEACE AND FOR GOOD BEHAVIOUR
nodes.summary: This chapter provides the legal framework for requiring individuals to provide security for maintaining peace and good behavior.

nodes.title: Security for keeping peace on conviction
nodes.summary: Allows courts to require individuals convicted of certain offenses to provide security for keeping the peace.

nodes.title: Security for keeping peace in other cases
nodes.summary: Empowers Executive Magistrates to require security from individuals likely to breach the peace.

nodes.title: Security for good behaviour from persons disseminating certain matters
nodes.summary: Allows for requiring security from individuals disseminating harmful or defamatory content.

nodes.title: Security for good behaviour from suspected persons
nodes.summary: Permits requiring security from individuals suspected of planning to commit cognizable offenses.

nodes.title: Security for good behaviour from habitual offenders
nodes.summary: Allows for requiring security from habitual offenders to ensure good behavior.

nodes.title: Order to be made
nodes.summary: Details the procedure for making an order requiring security from individuals.

nodes.title: Procedure in respect of person present in Court
nodes.summary: Outlines the procedure for handling individuals present in court when an order for security is made.

nodes.title: Summons or warrant in case of person not so present
nodes.summary: Describes the issuance of summons or warrants for individuals not present in court.

nodes.title: Copy of order to accompany summons or warrant
nodes.summary: Requires that a copy of the order accompany any summons or warrant issued.

nodes.title: Power to dispense with personal attendance
nodes.summary: Allows Magistrates to dispense with the personal attendance of individuals required to show cause.

nodes.title: Inquiry as to truth of information
nodes.summary: Mandates an inquiry into the truth of information leading to an order for security.

nodes.title: Order to give security
nodes.summary: Allows Magistrates to order individuals to provide security if necessary for peace or good behavior.

nodes.title: Discharge of person informed against
nodes.summary: Provides for the discharge of individuals if it is not proven that security is necessary.

nodes.title: Commencement of period for which security is required
nodes.summary: Specifies when the period for which security is required begins.

nodes.title: Contents of bond
nodes.summary: Details the contents required in a bond for keeping peace or good behavior.

nodes.title: Power to reject sureties
nodes.summary: Allows Magistrates to reject sureties deemed unfit for the purposes of a bond.

nodes.title: Imprisonment in default of security
nodes.summary: Provides for imprisonment if an individual fails to provide required security.

nodes.title: Power to release persons imprisoned for failing to give security
nodes.summary: Allows for the release of individuals imprisoned for failing to provide security under certain conditions.

nodes.title: Security for unexpired period of bond
nodes.summary: Requires fresh security for the unexpired portion of a bond if the original bond is canceled.

nodes.title: ORDER FOR MAINTENANCE OF WIVES, CHILDREN AND PARENTS
nodes.summary: This chapter outlines the legal provisions for ordering maintenance for wives, children, and parents.

nodes.title: Order for maintenance of wives, children and parents
nodes.summary: Allows Magistrates to order individuals to provide maintenance for their wives, children, and parents.

nodes.title: Procedure
nodes.summary: Describes the procedure for taking maintenance proceedings against individuals.

nodes.title: Alteration in allowance
nodes.summary: Allows for the alteration of maintenance allowances based on changes in circumstances.

nodes.title: Enforcement of order of maintenance
nodes.summary: Provides for the enforcement of maintenance orders by Magistrates.

nodes.title: MAINTENANCE OF PUBLIC ORDER AND TRANQUILLITY
nodes.summary: This chapter deals with the maintenance of public order and the dispersal of unlawful assemblies.

nodes.title: Dispersal of assembly by use of civil force
nodes.summary: Empowers authorities to disperse unlawful assemblies using civil force.

nodes.title: Use of armed forces to disperse assembly
nodes.summary: Allows for the use of armed forces to disperse assemblies when necessary for public security.

nodes.title: Power of certain armed force officers to disperse assembly
nodes.summary: Grants certain armed force officers the power to disperse assemblies when public security is endangered.

nodes.title: Protection against prosecution for acts done under sections 148, 149 and 150
nodes.summary: Provides protection against prosecution for actions taken in good faith under sections 148, 149, and 150.

nodes.title: Conditional order for removal of nuisance
nodes.summary: Empowers a Magistrate to issue a conditional order for the removal of public nuisances.

nodes.title: Service or notification of order
nodes.summary: Details the procedure for serving or notifying an order made under Section 152.

nodes.title: Person to whom order is addressed to obey or show cause
nodes.summary: Specifies the actions required by a person against whom an order is made under Section 152.

nodes.title: Penalty for failure to comply with section 154
nodes.summary: Outlines the penalties for failing to comply with an order under Section 154.

nodes.title: Procedure where existence of public right is denied
nodes.summary: Describes the procedure when a public right is denied in response to an order under Section 152.

nodes.title: Procedure where person against whom order is made under section 152 appears to show cause
nodes.summary: Details the procedure when a person appears to show cause against an order under Section 152.

nodes.title: Power of Magistrate to direct local investigation and examination of an expert
nodes.summary: Allows a Magistrate to direct local investigations or summon experts for inquiries.

nodes.title: Power of Magistrate to furnish written instructions, etc.
nodes.summary: Empowers a Magistrate to provide written instructions for local investigations.

nodes.title: Procedure on order being made absolute and consequences of disobedience
nodes.summary: Outlines the procedure and consequences when an order is made absolute.

nodes.title: Injunction pending inquiry
nodes.summary: Allows a Magistrate to issue an injunction to prevent imminent danger pending inquiry.

nodes.title: Magistrate may prohibit repetition or continuance of public nuisance
nodes.summary: Empowers a Magistrate to prohibit the repetition or continuance of a public nuisance.

nodes.title: Power to issue order in urgent cases of nuisance or apprehended danger
nodes.summary: Allows a Magistrate to issue orders in urgent cases of nuisance or danger.

nodes.title: Procedure where dispute concerning land or water is likely to cause breach of peace
nodes.summary: Describes the procedure for disputes over land or water likely to cause a breach of peace.

nodes.title: Power to attach subject of dispute and to appoint receiver
nodes.summary: Allows a Magistrate to attach disputed property and appoint a receiver.

nodes.title: Dispute concerning right of use of land or water
nodes.summary: Covers disputes regarding the right to use land or water.

nodes.title: Local inquiry
nodes.summary: Provides for local inquiries by subordinate Magistrates.

nodes.title: Police to prevent cognizable offences
nodes.summary: Mandates police officers to prevent the commission of cognizable offences.

nodes.title: Information of design to commit cognizable offences
nodes.summary: Requires police officers to communicate information about designs to commit cognizable offences.

nodes.title: Arrest to prevent commission of cognizable offences
nodes.summary: Allows police officers to arrest individuals to prevent the commission of cognizable offences.

nodes.title: Prevention of injury to public property
nodes.summary: Empowers police officers to prevent injury to public property.

nodes.title: Persons bound to conform to lawful directions of police
nodes.summary: Obligates individuals to follow lawful directions given by police officers.

nodes.title: Information in cognizable cases
nodes.summary: Details the procedure for providing information about cognizable offences to police.

nodes.title: Information as to non-cognizable cases and investigation of such cases
nodes.summary: Outlines the procedure for handling information about non-cognizable offences.

nodes.title: Police officer's power to investigate cognizable case
nodes.summary: Grants police officers the power to investigate cognizable cases.

nodes.title: Procedure for investigation
nodes.summary: Describes the procedure for police investigations of offences.

nodes.title: Report how submitted
nodes.summary: Specifies how police reports should be submitted to a Magistrate.

nodes.title: Power to hold investigation or preliminary inquiry
nodes.summary: Allows a Magistrate to hold an investigation or preliminary inquiry.

nodes.title: Police officer's power to require attendance of witnesses
nodes.summary: Empowers police officers to require the attendance of witnesses during investigations.

nodes.title: Examination of witnesses by police
nodes.summary: Allows police officers to examine witnesses during investigations.

nodes.title: Statements to police and use thereof
nodes.summary: Regulates the use of statements made to police during investigations.

nodes.title: No inducement to be offered
nodes.summary: Prohibits police from offering inducements during investigations.

nodes.title: Recording of confessions and statements
nodes.summary: Details the procedure for recording confessions and statements by Magistrates.

nodes.title: Medical examination of victim of rape
nodes.summary: Mandates medical examination of rape victims by registered medical practitioners.

nodes.title: Search by police officer
nodes.summary: Empowers police officers to conduct searches during investigations.

nodes.title: When officer in charge of police station may require another to issue search-warrant
nodes.summary: Allows a police officer to request another officer to issue a search warrant.

nodes.title: Procedure when investigation cannot be completed in twenty-four hours
nodes.summary: Outlines the procedure when an investigation cannot be completed within twenty-four hours.

nodes.title: Power to examine accused
nodes.summary: This section outlines the court's power to question the accused during an inquiry or trial.

nodes.title: Oral arguments and memorandum of arguments
nodes.summary: This section allows parties to present oral arguments and submit a memorandum of arguments.

nodes.title: Accused person to be competent witness
nodes.summary: This section states that an accused person can be a witness for the defense.

nodes.title: No influence to be used to induce disclosure
nodes.summary: This section prohibits using influence to induce an accused to disclose or withhold information.

nodes.title: Provision for inquiries and trial being held in absence of accused in certain cases
nodes.summary: This section allows trials to proceed in the absence of the accused under certain conditions.

nodes.title: Inquiry, trial or judgment in absentia of proclaimed offender
nodes.summary: This section provides for trials in absentia for proclaimed offenders who evade arrest.

nodes.title: Procedure where accused does not understand proceedings
nodes.summary: This section outlines the procedure when an accused cannot understand the proceedings.

nodes.title: Power to proceed against other persons appearing to be guilty of offence
nodes.summary: This section allows the court to proceed against other persons who appear guilty during a trial.

nodes.title: Compounding of offences
nodes.summary: This section lists offences that can be compounded and the persons who can compound them.

nodes.title: Withdrawal from prosecution
nodes.summary: This section allows the Public Prosecutor to withdraw from prosecution with court consent.

nodes.title: Procedure in cases which Magistrate cannot dispose of
nodes.summary: This section outlines the procedure when a Magistrate cannot dispose of a case.

nodes.title: Procedure when after commencement of inquiry or trial, Magistrate finds case should be committed
nodes.summary: This section provides for committing a case to the Court of Session if necessary.

nodes.title: Trial of persons previously convicted of offences against coinage, stamp-law or property
nodes.summary: This section deals with the trial of persons previously convicted of certain offences.

nodes.title: Procedure when Magistrate cannot pass sentence sufficiently severe
nodes.summary: This section outlines the procedure when a Magistrate cannot pass a sufficiently severe sentence.

nodes.title: Conviction or commitment on evidence partly recorded by one Magistrate and partly by another
nodes.summary: This section allows a succeeding Magistrate to act on evidence recorded by a predecessor.

nodes.title: Court to be open
nodes.summary: This section mandates that criminal courts be open to the public, with certain exceptions.

nodes.title: PROVISIONS AS TO ACCUSED PERSONS OF UNSOUND MIND
nodes.summary: This chapter outlines procedures for dealing with accused persons of unsound mind.

nodes.title: Procedure in case of accused being person of unsound mind
nodes.summary: This section outlines the procedure when an accused is suspected to be of unsound mind.

nodes.title: Procedure in case of person of unsound mind tried before Court
nodes.summary: This section provides the procedure for trials involving persons of unsound mind.

nodes.title: Release of person of unsound mind pending investigation or trial
nodes.summary: This section allows for the release of persons of unsound mind pending trial under certain conditions.

nodes.title: Resumption of inquiry or trial
nodes.summary: This section allows for the resumption of inquiry or trial once the accused is capable of defense.

nodes.title: Procedure on accused appearing before Magistrate or Court
nodes.summary: This section outlines the procedure when an accused appears capable of defense.

nodes.title: When accused appears to have been of sound mind
nodes.summary: This section deals with cases where the accused was of sound mind at the time of the act.

nodes.title: Judgment of acquittal on ground of unsoundness of mind
nodes.summary: This section provides for acquittal if the accused was of unsound mind at the time of the act.

nodes.title: Person acquitted on ground of unsoundness of mind to be detained in safe custody
nodes.summary: This section mandates safe custody for persons acquitted due to unsoundness of mind.

nodes.title: Power of State Government to empower officer in charge to discharge
nodes.summary: This section allows the State Government to empower officers to discharge certain functions.

nodes.title: Procedure where prisoner of unsound mind is reported capable of making his defence
nodes.summary: This section outlines the procedure when a prisoner is deemed capable of defense.

nodes.title: Procedure where person of unsound mind detained is declared fit to be released
nodes.summary: This section provides for the release of detained persons once they are fit.

nodes.title: Delivery of person of unsound mind to care of relative or friend
nodes.summary: This section allows for the delivery of persons of unsound mind to relatives or friends.

nodes.title: PROVISIONS AS TO OFFENCES AFFECTING THE ADMINISTRATION OF JUSTICE
nodes.summary: This chapter deals with offences affecting the administration of justice.

nodes.title: Procedure in cases mentioned in section 215
nodes.summary: This section outlines the procedure for offences related to court proceedings.

nodes.title: Appeal
nodes.summary: This section provides for appeals against court decisions on complaints.

nodes.title: Power to order costs
nodes.summary: This section allows courts to order costs in certain proceedings.

nodes.title: Procedure of Magistrate taking cognizance
nodes.summary: This section outlines the procedure for Magistrates taking cognizance of complaints.

nodes.title: Summary procedure for trial for giving false evidence
nodes.summary: This section provides a summary procedure for trying false evidence cases.

nodes.title: Procedure in certain cases of contempt
nodes.summary: This section outlines the procedure for contempt cases in court.

nodes.title: Procedure where Court considers that case should not be dealt with under section 384
nodes.summary: This section provides for forwarding contempt cases to a Magistrate if necessary.

nodes.title: When Registrar or Sub-Registrar to be deemed a Civil Court
nodes.summary: This section deems Registrars or Sub-Registrars as Civil Courts for certain purposes.

nodes.title: Discharge of offender on submission of apology
nodes.summary: This section allows for the discharge of offenders upon apology.

nodes.title: Imprisonment or committal of person refusing to answer or produce document
nodes.summary: This section provides for imprisonment of persons refusing to answer or produce documents.

nodes.title: Summary procedure for punishment for non-attendance by a witness in obedience to summons
nodes.summary: This section outlines the summary procedure for punishing non-attendance by witnesses.

nodes.title: Appeals from convictions under sections 383, 384, 388, and 389
nodes.summary: This section provides for appeals from convictions under specified sections.

nodes.title: Irregularities which do not vitiate proceedings
nodes.summary: This section outlines irregularities by a Magistrate that do not invalidate proceedings.

nodes.title: Irregularities which vitiate proceedings
nodes.summary: This section lists irregularities by a Magistrate that render proceedings void.

nodes.title: Proceedings in wrong place
nodes.summary: This section states that proceedings in the wrong location do not invalidate findings unless justice is compromised.

nodes.title: Non-compliance with provisions of section 183 or section 316
nodes.summary: This section allows courts to admit statements despite non-compliance with recording provisions if no prejudice is caused.

nodes.title: Effect of omission to frame, or absence of, or error in, charge
nodes.summary: This section addresses the impact of errors or omissions in charges on the validity of court findings.

nodes.title: Finding or sentence when reversible by reason of error, omission or irregularity
nodes.summary: This section discusses when findings or sentences can be reversed due to procedural errors.

nodes.title: Defect or error not to make attachment unlawful
nodes.summary: This section states that defects in procedure do not render an attachment unlawful.

nodes.title: Definitions
nodes.summary: This section defines 'period of limitation' for taking cognizance of offences.

nodes.title: Bar to taking cognizance after lapse of period of limitation
nodes.summary: This section sets the limitation periods for taking cognizance of offences.

nodes.title: Commencement of period of limitation
nodes.summary: This section outlines when the limitation period begins for an offender.

nodes.title: Exclusion of time in certain cases
nodes.summary: This section details circumstances under which time is excluded from the limitation period.

nodes.title: Exclusion of date on which Court is closed
nodes.summary: This section allows for the exclusion of days when the court is closed from the limitation period.

nodes.title: Continuing offence
nodes.summary: This section states that a fresh limitation period begins for continuing offences.

nodes.title: Extension of period of limitation in certain cases
nodes.summary: This section allows courts to extend the limitation period if justified by circumstances.

nodes.title: Trials before High Courts
nodes.summary: This section mandates High Courts to follow the same procedure as Sessions Courts in trials.

nodes.title: Delivery to commanding officers of persons liable to be tried by Court-martial
nodes.summary: This section provides for the delivery of military personnel to commanding officers for trial by Court-martial.

nodes.title: Forms
nodes.summary: This section allows for the use of forms in the Second Schedule with necessary variations.

nodes.title: Power of High Court to make rules
nodes.summary: This section empowers High Courts to make rules regarding petition-writers and other matters.

nodes.title: Power to alter functions allocated to Executive Magistrate in certain cases
nodes.summary: This section allows State Governments to reassign functions from Executive to Judicial Magistrates.

nodes.title: Cases in which Judge or Magistrate is personally interested
nodes.summary: This section prohibits judges or magistrates from trying cases in which they have a personal interest.

nodes.title: Practising advocate not to sit as Magistrate in certain Courts
nodes.summary: This section prohibits advocates from sitting as magistrates in courts where they practice.

nodes.title: Public servant concerned in sale not to purchase or bid for property
nodes.summary: This section prohibits public servants involved in property sales from purchasing or bidding.

nodes.title: Saving of inherent powers of High Court
nodes.summary: This section preserves the inherent powers of High Courts to ensure justice and prevent abuse of process.

nodes.title: Duty of High Court to exercise continuous superintendence over Courts
nodes.summary: This section mandates High Courts to supervise subordinate courts for efficient case disposal.

nodes.title: Trial and proceedings to be held in electronic mode
nodes.summary: This section allows trials and proceedings to be conducted electronically.

nodes.title: Repeal and savings
nodes.summary: This section repeals the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1973, and provides for transitional provisions.

nodes.title: BOND AND BAIL-BOND ON A PRELIMINARY INQUIRY BEFORE A POLICE OFFICER
nodes.summary: This section provides a form for a bond and bail-bond during a preliminary inquiry before a police officer.

nodes.title: BOND TO PROSECUTE OR GIVE EVIDENCE
nodes.summary: This section provides a form for a bond to prosecute or give evidence in a case.

nodes.title: SPECIAL SUMMONS TO A PERSON ACCUSED OF A PETTY OFFENCE
nodes.summary: This section provides a form for a special summons to a person accused of a petty offence.

nodes.title: NOTICE OF COMMITMENT BY MAGISTRATE TO PUBLIC PROSECUTOR
nodes.summary: This section provides a form for a magistrate's notice of commitment to the public prosecutor.

nodes.title: CHARGES
nodes.summary: This section provides forms for various charges under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023.

nodes.title: SUMMONS TO WITNESS
nodes.summary: This section provides a form for a summons to a witness to appear in court.
